nodes.label: 6:45 AM daily
nodes.notes: Fires at dawn, weekdays and weekends alike.
nodes.description: The day opens. A cron clock fires at 6:45 AM and wakes the workflow.

nodes.label: Inbox
nodes.description: Yesterday's residue — notes, commits, and drifts — waits to be swept up.

nodes.label: Rodbot
nodes.notes: Reads the trigger + inbox residue, writes the grid.
nodes.description: Rodbot reads yesterday's residue and decides what today should look like.

nodes.label: Reflection
nodes.notes: Turns raw state into 9 named cells.
nodes.description: Raw thinking becomes nine named cells, ready to render as the morning grid.

nodes.label: Activity
nodes.description: Every pass leaves a trace in the append-only activity ledger.

nodes.label: Morning grid
nodes.notes: The 3×3 that Jake sees at open.
nodes.description: The 3×3 morning grid lands in the UI — the first thing Jake sees.

nodes.label: Team Slack
nodes.notes: Briefs the team on the day's shape.
nodes.description: If the day has heat, the team gets a Slack brief on the shape of it.

connections.label: fire
connections.description: The dawn clock fires the workflow.

connections.label: read
connections.description: Rodbot reads the last 100 inbox lines as context.

connections.label: raw state
connections.description: Rodbot's raw thinking flows into the reflection schema.

connections.label: append
connections.description: The reflection is appended to the ledger as a durable audit line.
The nine cells render into the morning grid.

connections.label: if interesting
connections.description: Only post to Slack when at least one cell rates high priority.
connections.narration: Only when the morning grid surfaces at least one high-priority cell does the reflection step forward its condensed take to Team Slack — routing the day's signal to the team channel and staying silent on quiet mornings.
